steps.title: Spot the parallel linguistic-geographic pattern
steps.content: Mandarin is widely spoken in north China and is dominant in Flushing, Queens; Cantonese and Fuzhounese are widely spoken in south China and are dominant in Chinatown, Manhattan. This parallel structure linking regions to their dominant neighborhoods is the key clue.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the inference
steps.content:
The inference is straightforward: because regional Chinese languages dominate different New York City neighborhoods, it is reasonable to infer that people from the regions where those languages are widely spoken tend to settle in the corresponding neighborhoods.

This is exactly what choice B (‘people who emigrate from north China tend to settle in Queens, while people who emigrate from south China tend to settle in Manhattan.’) states. The other choices all require facts not in the passage—arrival timelines, speaker populations, or relocation behavior.

That confirms B is correct.
